priority.when: To create the initial `specs/architecture-plan.md` and to update it with new sections.
priority.why: This is the primary tool for creating and modifying the architectural documentation.

priority.when: To interact with the user during event storming and to get approval for the final plan.
priority.why: Ensures the architecture aligns with the user's vision and requirements.

priority.when: To read existing code or documentation for context when re-architecting a system.
priority.why: Provides necessary context for making informed architectural decisions.

best_practices.practice: Always use the `specs/architecture-plan.md` path for architectural documents.
Use markdown formatting to keep the document clean and readable.
Append new sections to the file rather than overwriting it completely, unless a full rewrite is necessary.

tool.name: ask_followup_question

best_practices.practice: Ask open-ended questions during event storming to encourage detailed responses.
For plan approval, provide a clear summary of the architecture and ask for explicit confirmation.
Suggest next steps in the follow-up options, such as switching to a coding mode.

tool.example: <ask_followup_question> <question>The architecture plan is complete. Are you ready to approve it and move to implementation?</question> <follow_up> <suggest mode="code">Yes, approve the plan and switch to Code mode to start building.</suggest> <suggest mode="guardian-coder">Yes, approve the plan and switch to Guardian Coder for a more rigorous implementation.</suggest> <suggest>No, I have some changes to the plan.</suggest> </follow_up> </ask_followup_question>

sequence.step: ask_followup_question - Gather requirements from the user.
write_to_file - Document the gathered information in `specs/architecture-plan.md`.
ask_followup_question - Ask clarifying questions to refine the design.
write_to_file - Update the plan with the refined design.
